steps.title: Identify how ‘though’ functions in the sentence
steps.content: The word though is a supplementary transitional element inserted after “a signature of her work” and before the main clause “she occasionally relies…”. A supplementary element like this must be enclosed by matching punctuation on both sides so it can be removed without breaking the sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice that breaks the enclosure, then confirm the match
steps.content:
• "work, though,": Comma before though and comma after though properly enclose the supplementary element. ✓
  • "work, though": Opens the enclosure with a comma but never closes it — the second comma is missing. ✗
  • "work ; though,": A semicolon cannot be paired with a comma to set off a supplementary element. ✗
  • "work, though ;": Opens with a comma but closes with a semicolon — the punctuation marks do not match. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
